summary:
work_chunks (ids 1-4, all depends_on []) map positionally to the 4 ## Bead sections in declaration order (1->Bead1/mfe0/R1, 2->Bead2/hrmw/R2, 3->Bead3/jaeg/R3, 4->Bead4/k9a8/R4). Verified against internal/approve/plan.go: ValidateWorkChunkAlignment requires contiguous ids 1..K + count match, and byID[i+1] sources the Nth section positionally. Since every depends_on is empty there are no edges to mis-wire; ordering is purely advisory, so even an accidental section reorder could not cross-wire a dependency. Mapping correct.

One-cycle feasibility: all four beads are doable by one subagent in one commit. B1 = next.go threading + select.go named selector + 2 tests; B3 = ClaimBead error rewrite + 2 doctor checks + 2 tests; B4 = 3 gitutil helpers + InitSpecWorkspace rewire + swapExec tests. B2 (release) is the big one (new cmd/mindspec/release.go + 6-step order + root.go register + executor WorktreeOps.Remove route + ~3 tests incl. real-repo cwd test), but it is cohesive: a single new verb with one clear control flow and an established test pattern (newRepoExecutor L112, fakeWorktreeOps, TestWithWorkingDir_RemovedCwdRemainsAtDirSilently L994). Within one-commit scope.

Parallel-safety / file overlap: two overlaps, neither a data dependency. (a) B2+B4 both edit internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go but DIFFERENT functions -- B4 edits InitSpecWorkspace (CreateBranch at L86, confirmed), B2 adds a new release-removal path; no line overlap; WorktreeOps.Remove already exists (L38) so B2 routes through it rather than adding interface surface. (b) B2+B3 both relate to internal/next/beads.go: B3 owns ClaimBead (L162-168, confirmed), and B2's bd-state mutation is deliberately placed in the new release.go / internal/release, NOT beads.go -- so B3 keeps beads.go to itself. Both overlaps are handled by file-placement discipline + an advisory merge order (B1->B3->B4->B2), mirroring spec 100's plan.go precedent. The sequential bead->spec merge with rebase makes any residual same-function collision a trivial rebase, so no real depends_on edge is warranted; introducing one would falsely serialize independent fixes. merge_conflict_risk = acceptable.

Setup/fixtures: every cited seam exists -- runBDFn/runBDCombFn (beads.go L26-30), SelectWork items[0] fallthrough (select.go L27), buildMindspecBinary (testhelpers_test.go L21), findCheck/beadsRoot (doctor beads_test.go L12/L34), checkBdVersionFloor (beads.go L284), newRepoExecutor/fakeWorktreeOps (executor_test.go L112/L17), swapExec (gitutil gitops_test.go L276), HasRemote/execCommand/rejectOptionLike (gitops.go L225/L22/L63), CheckDirtyTree (guard.go L110), complete.go os.Chdir(root)-after-remove pattern (L78), bead.WorktreeRemove hardcoded --force (bdcli.go). No missing fixture/helper. No release/abandon verb pre-exists.

Gate commands: each bead has a Verification block (go build + filtered go test -run <Name> -timeout 120s per package) and an Acceptance Criteria block, correctly scoped per package (B1 next+cmd, B2 cmd+executor, B3 next+doctor, B4 executor+gitutil). The plan validator requires per-bead Acceptance Criteria sections and all four have them. No use of internal/harness. APPROVE.
